perturbation.original_text: Section 1. Promotion Services. Promoter to provide the following services to VNUE in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth in this agreement: A. VNUE hereby engages the Promoter as an Independent Contractor to secure contracts for VNUE with Thirty (30) music venues.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 1. Promotion Services. Promoter to provide the following services to VNUE in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth in this agreement: A. VNUE hereby engages the Promoter as an Agent to secure contracts for VNUE with Thirty (30) music venues.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Independent Contractor' to 'Agent' introduces a contradiction. The agreement initially establishes the Promoter as an independent contractor in the introductory paragraphs. This change in Section 1A now classifies the Promoter as an 'Agent', implying a different legal relationship with potential authority to bind VNUE, which contradicts the original intent and the explicit statement in the first section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Independent Contractor. Nothing contained herein or any document executed in connection herewith, shall be construed to create an employer-employee, partnership or joint venture relationship between VNUE and Promoter. Promoter is an independent contractor and not an officer, director, affiliate, insider, employee or agent of VNUE or any of its subsidiaries or affiliates. Promoter has no authority to, and will not, enter into contracts, make representations, warranties or commitments purporting to be binding on VNUE or otherwise act on VNUE's behalf and shall not take any action that might lead third parties to believe Promoter has the right to do so. The consideration set forth in Section 3 shall be the sole consideration due Promoter for the services rendered hereunder. It is understood that VNUE will not withhold any amounts for payment of taxes from the compensation of Promoter hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Independent Contractor' with 'Agent'. The contradicted text explicitly states that the Promoter is 'not an... agent of VNUE' and 'has no authority to... enter into contracts... purporting to be binding on VNUE'. By changing the classification to 'Agent', the modified text directly contradicts the clause denying the Promoter any agency or contractual authority, creating a clear and impactful inconsistency within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Section 2. Promoter's Fee. For providing services as set forth herein, VNUE will compensate Promoter i) Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($2,500.00) for each One (1) Year contract Promoter secures per venue and Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000,00) for each Two (2) Year contract Promoter secures per venue, with payment due to Promoter within Thirty (30) Days from the date on which each such contract is countersigned; and ii) through the issuance of VNUE common stock as set forth below, and for the purposes of Rule 144 such shares of stock shall be deemed to have fully earned by Promoter upon the date of each issuance of such stock certificates by VStock Transfer: Three Million (3,000,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter for performing Promotion Services as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: Section 2. Promoter's Fee. For providing services as set forth herein, VNUE may provide monetary incentives to Promoter i) Up to Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($2,500.00) for each One (1) Year contract Promoter secures per venue and Up to Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000,00) for each Two (2) Year contract Promoter secures per venue, with payment due to Promoter within Sixty (60) Days from the date on which each such contract is countersigned; and ii) through the possible issuance of VNUE common stock as set forth below, and for the purposes of Rule 144 such shares of stock shall be deemed to have partially earned by Promoter upon the date of each issuance of such stock certificates by VStock Transfer: Up to Three Million (3,000,000) shares of VNUE common stock may be awarded to Promoter for performing Promotion Services as follows:
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will compensate' to 'may provide monetary incentives' and 'shall be awarded' to 'may be awarded', introduces a contradiction turning a mandatory compensation into a discretionary reward. Also, changing 'payment due to Promoter within Thirty (30) Days' to 'payment due to Promoter within Sixty (60) Days' introduces a different in the deadline of payment. This creates uncertainty and ambiguity as it contradicts the initial compensation agreement, leading to potential disputes over payment and stock issuance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2. Promoter's Fee. For providing services as set forth herein, VNUE may provide monetary incentives to Promoter i) Up to Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($2,500.00) for each One (1) Year contract Promoter secures per venue and Up to Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000,00) for each Two (2) Year contract Promoter secures per venue, with payment due to Promoter within Sixty (60) Days from the date on which each such contract is countersigned; and ii) through the possible issuance of VNUE common stock as set forth below, and for the purposes of Rule 144 such shares of stock shall be deemed to have partially earned by Promoter upon the date of each issuance of such stock certificates by VStock Transfer: Up to Three Million (3,000,000) shares of VNUE common stock may be awarded to Promoter for performing Promotion Services as follows:
perturbation.justification: The changed text and the contradicted text are identical. The original text contains language that creates a contradiction by changing 'will compensate' to 'may provide monetary incentives' and 'shall be awarded' to 'may be awarded'. Therefore, there is no contradiction between the changed text and the contradicted text, as the changed text becomes the contradicted text and are the same.

perturbation.original_text: Section 3. Expenses. VNUE shall reimburse Promoter for expenses incurred by Promoter while performing the duties herein. Promoter shall deliver to VNUE an itemized accounting of expenses incurred on a weekly basis, and VNUE shall reimburse Promoter within Thirty (30) Days of receipt of such accounting.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 3. Expenses. VNUE may reimburse Promoter for expenses incurred by Promoter while performing the duties herein, subject to VNUE's Expense Policy. Promoter shall deliver to VNUE an accounting of expenses incurred on a monthly basis, and VNUE will endeavor to reimburse Promoter within Forty-Five (45) Days of approval of such accounting, per VNUE's Expense Policy.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall reimburse' to 'may reimburse' and adding 'subject to VNUE's Expense Policy' introduces a significant ambiguity. It contradicts the initial obligation to reimburse by making it conditional. Additionally, the change from 'itemized accounting of expenses incurred on a weekly basis' to 'an accounting of expenses incurred on a monthly basis', and from 'shall reimburse Promoter within Thirty (30) Days of receipt of such accounting' to 'will endeavor to reimburse Promoter within Forty-Five (45) Days of approval of such accounting, per VNUE's Expense Policy.' creates further conflicting terms by dictating a certain Expense Policy which wasnt mentioned before, and a new timeframe for approval. This creates uncertainty about whether VNUE is truly obligated to cover expenses and under what conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3. Expenses. VNUE may reimburse Promoter for expenses incurred by Promoter while performing the duties herein, subject to VNUE's Expense Policy. Promoter shall deliver to VNUE an accounting of expenses incurred on a monthly basis, and VNUE will endeavor to reimburse Promoter within Forty-Five (45) Days of approval of such accounting, per VNUE's Expense Policy.
perturbation.justification: The changed text and the contradicted text are identical. While the perturbation explanation suggests a change from mandatory to conditional reimbursement and a change in timeframes that could potentially create a contradiction with other parts of a document where the initial obligation to reimburse or the original timeframe is referenced, the excerpts of the changed text and the contradicted text are exactly the same, meaning that there's no explicit contradiction between them as they are now. The changed terms are applied uniformly and dont contradict each other, just potentially introduce an element that requires comparison with another clause. Therefore, based only on the provided text, there's no in-text contradiction.
